content.full_text:
Fern Poltrtcel PRACTICEs C ortaulsSIoN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 11,2006
Don Watts, AIA
171 Adams Street
Sierra Madre, CA 91024
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-L26
Dear Mr. Watts:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Politic4l Reform Act (the "Act").1 The Act requires the
Comrnission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in
question. (Section 8311a@); regulation 18329(a), copy enclosed.) However, the
Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or
otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(bX8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section
83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we
decline to provide written advice to you at this time. Your current question involves an
analysis of a series of interrelated activities, some of which have already occurred.
Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further
the impartial, effective administration of the Act mandated by section 831 I 1.
In addition, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the same
or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the
Enforcement Division.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No.V/-06-126
Page No. 2
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. Moreover, nothing in the Act prevents you frorn seeking legal advice from
pivate counsel.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
VJ:rd
I:\Adviceltrs\W-06- I 26
